macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. and Israel Launch Major Military Operations Against Iran
items.summary: President Trump announced that the United States military began major combat operations in Iran early Saturday to eliminate threats from the Iranian regime and defend against nuclear ambitions. The strikes triggered immediate market volatility, with Bitcoin falling 3.1% and oil prices surging.

items.title: U.S. Stock Indices Close Lower Amid AI Concerns and Inflation Worries
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 0.4% to 6,878.88, the Dow dropped 1.1% to 48,977.92, and the Nasdaq sank 0.9% to 22,668.21 on Friday as investors punished software companies vulnerable to AI disruption and reacted to hotter-than-expected inflation data.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge on Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Benchmark U.S. crude rose 2.8% to $67.02 per barrel as traders anticipated military strikes against Iran, marking the latest swing in a market unsettled by U.S.-Iran nuclear tensions.

items.title: Netflix Surges 13.8% After Abandoning Warner Bros. Discovery Acquisition
items.summary: Netflix climbed 13.8% after walking away from its bid to acquire Warner Bros. Discovery's studio and streaming business, positioning Skydance-owned Paramount to take over the rival. Paramount Skydance shares jumped 20.8%.

items.title: ISM Manufacturing Index Returns to Expansion
items.summary: The ISM Manufacturing Index rose to 52.6 from 47.9, marking a return to expansion for the first time in 10 months with broad gains in new orders, production, and backlogs, partly driven by increased capital spending from restored 100% bonus depreciation incentives.

items.title: Treasury Yields Fall as Safe-Haven Assets Rally
items.summary: The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield broke below 4.0% for the first time since November 2025, on track for its best monthly performance in a year, while gold and silver spot prices moved higher to around $5,250 and $94 per ounce respectively amid rising global risks.

items.title: Private Equity Stocks Plunge on AI Disruption Concerns
items.summary: Apollo Global Management dropped 8.6% and Blue Owl Capital fell 6% as investors worried about loans made to software companies that could be disrupted by AI competitors, reflecting broader concerns about private credit exposure.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Dips Toward $60K as Crypto Market Crashes Amid Macro Pressures and Liquidations
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped over 6% in the past 24 hours to near $60,000, with Ethereum falling nearly 10% to around $1,800, triggered by hotter-than-expected January 2026 PPI data, panic selling, and $88.13 million in BTC liquidations. Altcoins bled across the board amid deteriorating macro backdrop and reduced Spot Bitcoin ETF inflows.

items.title: Bitcoin Tumbles to $63,561 Following Trump's Announcement of Massive Strikes on Iran
items.summary: Bitcoin slid 3.1% to $63,561 after U.S. President Trump announced a 'massive' strike against Iran, with explosions reported in Tehran near Supreme Leader's offices, signaling rocky markets ahead as oil and gold gain while equities brace for hits. Cryptocurrencies provided early glimpse of investor reaction in 24/7 trading.

items.title: Crypto Market in 'Risk-Off' Mode Amid Regulatory Anticipation and Heightened Security Risks
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market ended the week in consolidation with profit-taking into large assets, reacting to tech sector reassessment, upcoming regulatory decisions, and sensitivity to hacks in bridges and smart contracts. Investors advised to monitor regulators' rhetoric, ETF flows, liquidity, and risk signals over next 24-72 hours.

items.title: Crypto Winter Deepens: Bitcoin Down 48.5% from ATH, Stablecoins Pegged to Real Assets Seen as Future
items.summary: Bitcoin hovered above $65,000, marking a 48.5% drawdown from its $126,272 all-time high last October, entering fourth crypto winter since 2009, with 'Bitcoin to zero' searches spiking. Survival demands reform toward real-asset-anchored stablecoins, whose $300B market cap is poised to surpass Bitcoin-style cryptos soon.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: India Announces Three Domestically-Built AI Models
items.summary: India launched three new domestically-developed AI models, marking a significant step in its AI self-reliance efforts. This development was highlighted in recent AI updates.

items.title: VoiceBox Released as Open-Source Local-First AI Voice Cloning Tool
items.summary: VoiceBox, a new open-source tool for local AI voice cloning, was introduced, enabling privacy-focused voice synthesis without cloud dependency.

items.title: GGML.ai Acquired by Hugging Face
items.summary: Hugging Face has acquired GGML.ai, potentially enhancing its capabilities in efficient machine learning model deployment and inference.

items.title: David Silver Launches Ineffable Intelligence Startup
items.summary: Renowned AI researcher David Silver started Ineffable Intelligence, a new venture focused on advanced world models in AI.

items.title: Taalas Chips Achieve 17,000 Tokens per Second Inference
items.summary: Taalas unveiled chips capable of running AI models at an unprecedented 17,000 tokens per second without relying on CPU or GPU.

items.title: Chinese Firms Distill Anthropic's Claude Models
items.summary: Chinese AI companies have successfully distilled models from Anthropic's Claude, advancing local capabilities in high-performance language models.

items.title: Claude AI Coding in COBOL Triggers IBM Shares Crash
items.summary: An instance of Anthropic's Claude coding in COBOL reportedly caused a sharp drop in IBM shares, valued at $31 billion.

items.title: Google Builds Self-Learning AI with RL2F
items.summary: Google developed a self-learning AI system using Reinforcement Learning 2F (RL2F), pushing boundaries in autonomous model improvement.

items.title: Basis Raises $100M Series B for Agentic AI Accounting Platform
items.summary: Agentic AI accounting platform Basis secured $100 million in Series B funding at a $1.15 billion valuation, highlighting investor interest in AI-driven fintech.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Pokémon Presents Reveals Major 30th Anniversary Announcements Including New Switch 2 Games
items.summary: The February 2026 Pokémon Presents showcased a Game Music Collection jukebox, Pokémon Trading Card Game anniversary set, Pokémon XD: Gale of Darkness on Nintendo Switch Online, FireRed and LeafGreen releases on Switch, Pokémon Champions in April, and teases for new 2027 Switch 2 titles Pokemon Winds and Pokemon Waves with open-world exploration.[2][3][4]
items.source: Game Informer, Japan Times, Nintendo Life

items.title: ULTRAKILL Releases Layer 8: Fraud After Two Years of Development
items.summary: ULTRAKILL launched its highly anticipated Layer 8: Fraud update featuring mind-bending environments with shifting hallways and portals, receiving positive early reception for initial levels despite some criticism on later stages.[1]

items.title: Tales of Berseria Remastered Launches Today
items.summary: Bandai Namco released Tales of Berseria Remastered, an enhanced version of the action RPG highlighting an epic tale torn between reason and emotion.[5]

items.title: ARC Raiders Deploys Shrouded Sky Update with New Threats and Rewards
items.summary: ARC Raiders introduced the Shrouded Sky update, adding new threats and limited-time rewards to keep the momentum going.[1]

items.title: Neverness to Everness Confirms April Release Date
items.summary: Neverness to Everness locked in its official April release date, ramping up excitement alongside a star-studded voice cast reveal for Crimson Desert.[1]

items.title: Ubisoft CEO Confirms Assassin's Creed and Far Cry Sequels to Lead Post-Reset Roadmap
items.summary: Ubisoft's CEO announced that sequels to Assassin's Creed and Far Cry will headline the company's roadmap following its reset.[1]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
